purpose: Shared cross-language fixture for local worker/service execution semantics. Runtimes must expose stateless worker loops that recover expired leases, execute claimable steps through Runtime.run_once, stop on terminal status, and report idle/stop summaries without owning durable state.

required_scenarios.name: local_worker_runs_until_terminal
required_scenarios.description: A local worker repeatedly claims and executes a run until it reaches a terminal status.

required_scenarios.name: worker_service_stops_after_idle_poll
required_scenarios.description: A service loop with no claimable work exits after max idle polls and reports idle state.

required_scenarios.name: worker_loop_recovers_expired_leases
required_scenarios.description: A worker loop recovers an expired lease and fences the previous owner before the replacement attempt commits.

preview_status: implemented by Go, TypeScript, and Rust preview runtimes as local worker/service parity; distributed scheduler backends remain optional adapters
